$comment: Declarative execution policy — the SINGLE source of truth for what an agent may run in this repo (étude 25 D-4). `npm run harness:sync` compiles it into per-tool views (today: .claude/settings.json); `npm run harness:check` fails CI if a view drifts from this file. Tools with no repo-level permission file (Codex, Cursor, …) read the human-readable summary in AGENTS.md § Execution policy instead — for them it is guidance, not enforcement; the hard net stays the husky hooks + required CI checks + the absence of prod credentials locally (étude 25 P-3/P-4).

allow.$comment: Always allowed — read-only inspection or the project's own gates. Grouped by intent so a reviewer sees WHY a rule exists, not just a flat list.

deny.reason: Prod migrates ONLY via db-migrate-prod.yml on merge (DoD §7) — a manual push strands prod behind code or applies unreviewed SQL.
Destructive: drops and recreates the database.
Same as the npx form — prod migrates only via db-migrate-prod.yml (DoD §7).
Same as the npx form — destructive.
